amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: The yeast mating response is a classic MAPK cascade triggered by pheromones. α-factor (from MATα cells) or a-factor (from MATa cells) bind GPCRs (Ste2/Ste3), activating Gα (Gpa1) dissociation from Gβγ (Ste4/Ste18). Free Gβγ recruits scaffold Ste5, which assembles the MAPK cascade: Ste11 (MAPKKK), Ste7 (MAPKK), Fus3/Kss1 (MAPK). Activated Fus3 phosphorylates transcription factor Ste12, relieves Far1-mediated repression, induces mating genes, and causes G1 arrest for shmoo formation.

graph TD %% Pheromone Detection A[Pheromone α-factor or a-factor] --> B{OR: Which
Receptor?} B -->|MATa cells| C[Ste2 GPCR binds α-factor] B -->|MATα cells| D[Ste3 GPCR binds a-factor] C --> E[Ste2 Conformational Change] D --> E %% G Protein Activation E --> F[Gpa1-GDP Gβγ Heterotrimer] F --> G[GEF Activity] G --> H[Gpa1-GTP Formation] H --> I[G Protein Dissociation] I --> J[\Gpa1-GTP Inactive/] I --> K[Gβγ Ste4/Ste18 Active] %% Scaffold Recruitment K --> L[Ste5 Scaffold Recruitment] L --> M{AND: Ste5 + Gβγ
Membrane?} M -->|No| N[No Signaling] M -->|Yes| O[Ste5 Membrane-Bound] %% MAPKKK Layer O --> P[Ste11 MAPKKK Recruitment] P --> Q{AND: Ste11 + Ste5
+ Ste50?} Q -->|No| N Q -->|Yes| R[Ste11 Complex] R --> S[Ste20 PAK Kinase] S --> T[Ste20 Phosphorylates Ste11] T --> U[Ste11 Active] %% MAPKK Layer U --> V[Ste7 MAPKK on Ste5] V --> W{AND: Ste7 + Ste5
Scaffold?} W -->|No| N W -->|Yes| X[Ste11 Phosphorylates Ste7] X --> Y[Ste7 Activated] %% MAPK Layer Y --> Z{OR: Which
MAPK?} Z -->|Fus3 - Mating| AA[Fus3 MAPK] Z -->|Kss1 - Invasion| AB[Kss1 MAPK] AA --> AC[Ste7 Phosphorylates Fus3] AC --> AD[Fus3 Dual Phosphorylation] AD --> AE[Fus3-PP Active] %% Fus3 Substrates AE --> AF{OR: Which
Substrate?} AF -->|Ste12| AG[Ste12 Transcription Factor] AF -->|Far1| AH[/Far1 CDK Inhibitor/] AF -->|Ste5| AI[Ste5 Feedback] AF -->|Msg5| AJ[/Msg5 Phosphatase/] %% Transcriptional Response AG --> AK{AND: Ste12
AND Dig1/2 Relief?} AK -->|No| AL[\Repressed/] AK -->|Yes| AM[Dig1/2 Phosphorylated] AM --> AN[Dig1/2 Release] AN --> AO[Ste12 Active] AO --> AP[Ste12 Binds PRE Elements] AP --> AQ{OR: Which
Mating Gene?} AQ -->|FUS1| AR[FUS1 Cell Fusion] AQ -->|FUS2| AS[FUS2 Cell Fusion] AQ -->|AGA1/2| AT(\1) AQ -->|FAR1| AU[FAR1 G1 Arrest] AQ -->|FIG1| AV[FIG1 Projection] %% Cell Cycle Arrest AH --> AW[Far1 Accumulation] AW --> AX{AND: Far1 + Cdc28-Cln?} AX -->|No| AY[Cell Cycle Continues] AX -->|Yes| AZ[\Cdc28-Cln Inhibited/] AZ --> BA[G1 Arrest] BA --> BB[Polarized Growth] BB --> BC[Shmoo Formation] %% Morphological Changes BC --> BD{AND: Actin
Polarization?} BD -->|No| BE[No Projection] BD -->|Yes| BF[Actin Cables to Tip] BF --> BG[Bni1 Formin] BG --> BH[Projection Growth] %% Cell Fusion Preparation AR --> BI[Fus1 Membrane Fusion Protein] AS --> BI AT --> BJ[Agglutinin Cell Adhesion] BJ --> BK[Partner Cell Contact] BK --> BL{AND: Both Cells
Shmoo?} BL -->|No| BM[Mating Fails] BL -->|Yes| BN[Cell Fusion Initiation] %% Cell Wall Degradation BN --> BO[/Local Wall Degradation/] BO --> BP{AND: Fus1 + Fus2
Present?} BP -->|No| BM BP -->|Yes| BQ[Membrane Fusion] BQ --> BR[Cytoplasm Mixing] BR --> BS[Nuclear Migration] BS --> BT(\1) BT --> BU([Diploid Zygote]) %% Negative Feedback AI --> BV[Ste5 Phosphorylation] BV --> BW[Ste5 Membrane Release] BW --> BX[Signaling Attenuation] AJ --> BY[Msg5 Dephosphorylates Fus3] BY --> BZ[Fus3 Inactivation] %% Styling %% Semantic Color Styling %% Semantic name: MAPK Pheromone Response Pathway (Mating)
notes: Shows very high AND gates (10) for MAPK cascade assembly and cell fusion checkpoints.
scientificAccuracy: Based on Nobel Prize-related work on GPCR signaling and extensive yeast mating research.

sources.title: Signal Transduction by the Pheromone-Responsive G Protein-Coupled Receptor and Associated MAP Kinase Cascade in Saccharomyces cerevisiae
FUS3, a kinase required for cell fusion in Saccharomyces cerevisiae
sources.authors: Elion EA, Brill JA, Fink GR.

sources.title: Ste5, a Scaffold Protein That Recruits Upstream Components for the Activation of the Pheromone Response MAPK Cascade in Yeast
sources.authors: Choi KY, Satterberg B, Elion EA.
